questions.question: What problem does <Teleport> solve for modals and tooltips?
options: It lazy-loads the modal component to reduce initial bundle size
It renders content outside the component's DOM subtree, avoiding parent CSS issues like overflow:hidden
It allows modals to share state with their parent component
It prevents modals from triggering Vue's reactivity system

questions.question: What is a Fragment in Vue 3?
options: A lazy-loaded component that loads on demand
A component wrapper that provides error boundaries
The ability to have multiple root elements in a template without a wrapping div
A virtual node used internally by the virtual DOM

questions.question: What caveat exists when using Fragments (multiple root elements) with fallthrough attributes?
options: Fallthrough attributes are completely disabled for multi-root components
Attributes don't apply automatically — you must bind $attrs explicitly since Vue doesn't know which root to target
All fallthrough attributes are applied to the first root element
Multi-root components cannot receive fallthrough attributes at all
